title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brookline's Question 1 passed with nearly 60% approval, securing 8,859 votes for and 5,826 against.
The approved tax override generates $23 million, allocating $18 million for schools and over $5 million for town services.
The decision prevents cutting 240 jobs and maintains key public safety and school programs in the town.

title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are evaluating their options for a No. 1 center amid uncertainty and potential internal growth from young players.
James Hagens and Fraser Minten, both young prospects, may develop into top-line centers, though patience is required.
Current potential solutions include a high-risk trade for an established player or nurturing existing talent into top performers.

title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Bouchard, 63, was arrested for the 1987 murder of his girlfriend, Alice Hawkes, in Westbrook, Maine.
The Maine Attorney General seeks to charge Bouchard with murder after a 2025 reinvestigation of the cold case.
Bouchard is currently held at Cumberland County Jail pending arraignment; information is sought from the public.

title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Sharp Football Analysis ranks the Patriots' 2026 schedule as the 12th-easiest in the NFL.
The Patriots will face eight teams that made the playoffs in 2025, including the Seahawks and Jaguars.
Matchups against weaker teams like the Jets, Dolphins, and Raiders improve the Patriots' schedule ranking.

title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs, ex-Patriots wideout, is a free agent after being released to free up cap space.
Diggs surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in 2025 but remains unsigned despite his productive season.
Reporter Phil Perry doubts a Patriots reunion with Diggs, citing potential A.J. Brown trade interest.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, died from gunshot wounds at a New Bedford hospital after being shot inside his car.
The shooting incident occurred on May 6 near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street, detected by ShotSpotter.
The homicide is under investigation by State Police and New Bedford police, announced the DA's office.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Candidates Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion each gathered enough signatures for the Suffolk DA primary ballot.
Rollins leads in Boston signatures with 1,430, while Hayden has the most campaign funds at over $60,000.
The primary on September 1 will reduce the contest to two candidates before the November general election.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah topped Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs list with $280.6 million in stock awards.
Vincent Roche, CEO of Analog Devices, was second on the list with $27,456,265 total compensation in 2025.
The Boston Business Journal analyzed stock awards, options, bonuses, and salaries from SEC proxy statements.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 2021 Lexus LX 570 linked to Karen Read is set for auction on May 14 in Woburn.
Readers predict bids from below market value to over $500,000, reflecting the SUV's trial notoriety.
Auction requires in-person attendance, registration with valid ID, and a $20,000 bank funds deposit.

title: ‘What happened today cannot stand’: Memorial Drive shooter fired more than 50 rounds before trooper, civilian intervened, DA says
author: Abby Patkin and Molly Farrar
bullets: Shooter Tyler Brown fired over 50 rounds at cars and pedestrians on Memorial Drive, injuring two men.
A State Police trooper and a Marine veteran stopped the shooter by injuring him in the extremities.
Brown faces charges of armed assault with intent to murder and firearm offenses; victims hospitalized with life-threatening injuries.
